reports.raw_text:
MRI KNEE REPORT (Extracted)
Patient: Yash Rajdeo
Age/Sex: 21 / Male
Findings:
- Anterior cruciate ligament shows Grade III tear with edema and fiber discontinuity.
- Posterior cruciate ligament appears mildly buckled.
- Complex tear in medial meniscus with bucket handle tear indication.
- Mild to moderate joint effusion with myofascitis.
- Low-grade interstitial tear in medial patellar retinaculum (MPFL).
- Mild prepatellar bursitis.
- Other knee structures appear normal.
Impression:
- Grade III ACL tear
- Complex medial meniscus tear (bucket handle)
- Joint effusion
- MPFL tear
Recommendation:
Please correlate clinically and consult orthopedic specialist.
reports.analysis:
🔍 REPORT TYPE: Orthopedic MRI Report

1️⃣ HIGH/ABNORMAL VALUES 🔺
- Anterior cruciate ligament | Grade III tear | Normal: Intact | Possible cause: Trauma, injury | Precaution: Avoid weight-bearing activities
- Posterior cruciate ligament | Mildly buckled | Normal: Straight | Possible cause: Mild injury, strain | Precaution: Rest and ice
- Medial meniscus | Complex tear (bucket handle) | Normal: Intact | Possible cause: Trauma, injury | Precaution: Avoid twisting and bending
- Joint effusion | Mild to moderate | Normal: Minimal | Possible cause: Injury, inflammation | Precaution: Rest and elevate
- Medial patellar retinaculum (MPFL) | Low-grade interstitial tear | Normal: Intact | Possible cause: Trauma, injury | Precaution: Avoid weight-bearing activities
- Prepatellar bursitis | Mild | Normal: Absent | Possible cause: Friction, pressure | Precaution: Wear proper padding

2️⃣ LOW VALUES 🔻
- None

3️⃣ NORMAL VALUES ✅
- Other knee structures appear normal

4️⃣ KEY FINDINGS
- Grade III ACL tear
- Complex medial meniscus tear (bucket handle)
- Joint effusion
- MPFL tear

5️⃣ LIFESTYLE TIPS
- Diet: Balanced and nutritious
- Hydration: Adequate
- Sleep: 7-8 hours
- Exercise: Avoid high-impact activities, focus on low-impact exercises

6️⃣ SEE A DOCTOR
- Specialist: Orthopedic specialist
- Urgency: High

7️⃣ RISK LEVEL: 🔴 High
